stem: A public health study surveys 20 residents surveyed and observes 9 positive outcomes out of 20 (45.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (13 observations, rate = 38.5%) and Group B (7 observations, rate = 57.1%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 44% and 56% of the population. Estimate the true vaccination coverage and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.45. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.4807 (range [0.4698, 0.4916]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.2582, 0.6579]. Point estimate: 0.4807.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.5: 0.5×0.39 + 0.5×0.5714 = 0.4807.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=20, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
